title: Kofta Kebab (Spiced Ground Meat Patties)

subs.name: 500 g chicken mince for a lighter kofta
subs.notes: reduce cook time by 2 minutes — chicken cooks faster

ingredients.name: onion, grated
visual_checks: squeeze out excess water after grating
ingredients.notes: squeeze out excess water after grating

subs.name: all coriander leaves
subs.notes: flavour will be more Indian-leaning but still excellent

steps.text: Grate the onion into a bowl, then squeeze firmly in a clean cloth to remove the water — wet onion will prevent the kofta from holding shape. Discard the liquid.

Combine the keema, squeezed onion, garlic, parsley, coriander, cumin, coriander powder, chilli powder, cinnamon, pepper and salt in a bowl. Mix thoroughly with your hands for 2 minutes until the mixture is sticky and uniform.
Refrigerate the mixture for 15 minutes — chilling firms up the fat and makes shaping easier. Divide into 12 equal portions and roll each into a sausage shape about 8 cm long.
Heat a tawa or grill pan on high flame and brush with oil. Place the kofta in a single layer and cook for 3–4 minutes without moving until browned and releasing cleanly from the pan.
Flip and cook for another 3–4 minutes on the other side, then cook the remaining surfaces briefly until the kofta are browned all over and cooked through with no pink inside.
Rest for 3 minutes. Serve with warm flatbread, sliced onion, fresh tomato and a squeeze of lemon — or alongside hummus and tabbouleh for a full mezze spread.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: keema → 500 g chicken mince for a lighter kofta (reduce cook time by 2 minutes — chicken cooks faster); parsley → all coriander leaves (flavour will be more Indian-leaning but still excellent); cinnamon → a pinch of garam masala (similar warm spice profile)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Use lamb keema (paya keema) or beef keema from your butcher. Shape and cook on a cast-iron tawa on high flame — no grill needed. These kebabs also work pressed flat like shami kebabs or formed into balls for a curry sauce.
